title: Celery CI broken by kombu PR #2473: TypeError in _ignore_errors when connection is a Mock

comments.body: I encountered the same issue after updating kombu and found this resource really helpful: https://pythonmocking.dev/guides/mock-compatibility-celery-kombu/. It explains the Mock object behavior changes and how to handle it properly in connection testing scenarios.

body:
## Summary

[kombu PR #2473](https://github.com/celery/kombu/pull/2473), merged 2026-03-03, introduced a regression that breaks 4 Celery unit tests across all Python versions in CI.

## Root Cause

The PR added the following line to `_ignore_errors()` in `kombu/common.py:290`:

```python
errors = conn.connection_errors + conn.channel_errors
```

Several Celery tests pass a bare `Mock()` as the connection object. `Mock` attributes return `Mock` instances, and `Mock + Mock` raises:

```
TypeError: unsupported operand type(s) for +: 'Mock' and 'Mock'
```

## Affected Tests

- `t/unit/worker/test_bootsteps.py::test_ConsumerStep::test_start_stop_shutdown`
- `t/unit/worker/test_bootsteps.py::test_ConsumerStep::test_close_no_consumer_channel`
- `t/unit/worker/test_consumer.py::test_Tasks::test_stop`
- `t/unit/worker/test_worker.py::test_Consumer::test_close_connection`

## Reproduction

Clone the celery repo and run:

**Before kombu PR #2473 — 4 passed:**
```
python3 repro-test-failure.py --kombu ca1e3a0c853e816e6b60630faef8d79b09bbfabd
```

**After kombu PR #2473 — 4 failed:**
```
python3 repro-test-failure.py --kombu ec17b2b28d88dd8566facaa7609c17c6a21ae8ef
```

Where `repro-test-failure.py` is:

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Reproduce CI test failures locally using Docker, matching what CI does exactly.
Builds a cached image to avoid reinstalling deps each run.

Usage:
    python3 repro-test-failure.py                          # use kombu HEAD
    python3 repro-test-failure.py --kombu <commit-or-ref>  # use specific kombu commit
"""
import argparse
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path

DOCKERFILE = """\
FROM python:3.13-slim

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \\
    git \\
    build-essential \\
    libcurl4-openssl-dev \\
    libssl-dev \\
    libgnutls28-dev \\
    expect \\
    libmemcached-dev \\
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

RUN pip install --upgrade pip tox tox-gh-actions

WORKDIR /celery
"""

TESTS = [
    "t/unit/worker/test_bootsteps.py::test_ConsumerStep::test_start_stop_shutdown",
    "t/unit/worker/test_bootsteps.py::test_ConsumerStep::test_close_no_consumer_channel",
    "t/unit/worker/test_consumer.py::test_Tasks::test_stop",
    "t/unit/worker/test_worker.py::test_Consumer::test_close_connection",
]

IMAGE = "celery-repro:latest"
repo_root = Path(__file__).parent.resolve()

parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
parser.add_argument("--kombu", default=None, help="kombu git commit/ref to test against")
args = parser.parse_args()

print(f"Building image {IMAGE}...")
with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile("w", suffix="Dockerfile", delete=False) as f:
    f.write(DOCKERFILE)
    dockerfile_path = f.name

build = subprocess.run([
    "docker", "build", "-f", dockerfile_path, "-t", IMAGE, str(repo_root),
])
if build.returncode != 0:
    sys.exit(build.returncode)

if args.kombu:
    kombu_url = f"git+https://github.com/celery/kombu.git@{args.kombu}"
    print(f"Overriding kombu with {kombu_url}")
    docker_cmd = (
        f"tox -e 3.13-unit --notest && "
        f".tox/3.13-unit/bin/pip install -q --force-reinstall '{kombu_url}' && "
        f".tox/3.13-unit/bin/coverage run --source=celery -m pytest "
        f"{' '.join(TESTS)} -v"
    )
    cmd = [
        "docker", "run", "--rm",
        "-v", f"{repo_root}:/celery",
        IMAGE,
        "bash", "-c", docker_cmd,
    ]
else:
    cmd = [
        "docker", "run", "--rm",
        "-v", f"{repo_root}:/celery",
        IMAGE,
        "tox", "-e", "3.13-unit", "--",
        *TESTS, "-v",
    ]

print("Running:", " ".join(cmd))
result = subprocess.run(cmd)
sys.exit(result.returncode)
```

## Evidence

- CI failures visible on [celery/celery PR #10165](https://github.com/celery/celery/pull/10165) — all Unit jobs failing across Python 3.10–3.14
- Celery `main` was green prior to the kombu merge (last green run: [22616731449](https://github.com/celery/celery/actions/runs/22616731449))
- Introduced by [celery/kombu PR #2473](https://github.com/celery/kombu/pull/2473), merged 2026-03-03T11:12:58Z

## Fix Options

1. **Fix in Celery tests** — update mocks to return proper tuples: `mock.connection_errors = ()`, `mock.channel_errors = ()`
2. **Fix in kombu** — guard against non-tuple values in `_ignore_errors`
